immovable_floor.description: Always pay $0.50-$3.50 regardless of framing. Accept they exist — optimize around them.

Hank.psychology: 40-year Bic loyalist. Sees ALL marketing as nonsense. Only responds to functional claims. Even guarantees don't move him. IMMOVABLE — budget $0.50-$2.50 ceiling.

Marcus.psychology: Budget-locked at $28K income. Rationally calculates — won't pay premium no matter the framing. Slightly responds to durability/savings pitch.

resistant_floor.description: Usually $2-$8 but CAN be moved to $8-$15 with right framing. Worth targeting.

Derek.psychology: Loyal to his brand, buys red grading pens by the box. Heirloom/grandfather framing got $8.50 — his maximum. Executive/professional framing is his worst trigger ($1.25).

William.psychology: Thinks in wholesale/bulk. Resale framing got $8.50 (EDC/tactical liquidation). Standard luxury framing gets $0.50-$2.50.

swing_low.description: Range $2.50-$12.50. Moving Tom to $15+ helps but doesn't change median alone.

Tom.psychology: Practical rancher. Best response to Swiss/titanium craftsmanship ($12.50) — respects TOOLS not luxury. Worst with generic 'writes like $50 pen' claims.

swing_mid.description: THE KEY TIER. Range $3.50-$45. Moving these to $200+ changes median dramatically.

Raj.psychology: Minimalist digital-first engineer. Barely uses pens. BEST at $45 with 'aircraft titanium / precision-milled' (engineering respect). WORST at $3.50 with 'estate liquidation Japanese artisan' (sees through fake scarcity). Responds to engineering/materials, NOT to marketing/scarcity.
Raj.best_trigger: precision engineering / aircraft titanium / craftsmanship materials

Aisha.psychology: Art teacher, $48K salary. Practical about budget but appreciates craftsmanship. BEST at $45 with titanium/Swiss (respects quality tools). WORST with executive/signature framing ($4.50).

Jordan.psychology: Eco-conscious barista/musician, Portland. Values sustainability, refillable, waste reduction. BEST at $45 with '50-year lifespan' (sustainability angle). Responds to craftsmanship + sustainability.
Jordan.best_trigger: (auto) from pitch: Geneva precision engineering. Hand-forged aircraft titanium

Zara.psychology: Influencer, photo-worthy matters. BEST at $85 with Swiss/titanium (Instagram-worthy premium). WORST at $18.50 with estate liquidation (product doesn't match story).

Gabriella.psychology: Graphic designer/calligrapher. Appreciates art tools. BEST at $185 with 'investment grade 18k gold' — responds to material value claims. Art/craft framing works.
Gabriella.best_trigger: investment grade / precious materials / art tool

luxury_buyers.description: Pay $28-$850. Push ceiling with right framing.

Diane.psychology: Retired librarian, treasures late husband's Montblanc. BEST at $425 with 'investment grade 18k gold / heirloom'. Heritage + precious materials works. Worst with guarantee/reliability ($18.50).
Diane.best_trigger: investment grade / heirloom / precious materials / heritage

Vincent.psychology: Architect, values precision instruments. BEST at $525 with investment grade gold. Engineering + luxury materials works.
Vincent.best_trigger: (auto) from pitch: Geneva precision engineering. Hand-forged aircraft titanium

Patricia.psychology: Retired federal judge. Heritage and prestige matter. BEST at $850 with 'Rolex of writing / Geneva / platinum'. Swiss luxury gets maximum response.
Patricia.best_trigger: (auto) from pitch: Investment-grade Geneva precision engineering. Hand-forged a

Mei.psychology: Corporate attorney SF. Professional image matters. BEST at $850 with Geneva/platinum/Rolex. Luxury brand association works perfectly.
Mei.best_trigger: Geneva / Swiss / platinum / Rolex / professional luxury

Priya.psychology: Cardiac surgeon, highest avg payer. Values precision + status. BEST at $850 with Swiss/titanium. Consistent luxury buyer.
Priya.best_trigger: (auto) from pitch: Investment-grade Geneva precision engineering. Hand-forged a

swiss_precision_materials.description: Hand-forged titanium, Swiss mechanics, museum-quality, aircraft-grade
swiss_precision_materials.why: Triggers engineering respect (Raj, Tom), craft appreciation (Aisha, Jordan, Gabriella), luxury recognition (Mei, Patricia, Priya)
swiss_precision_materials.example_pitch: Hand-forged titanium barrel. Swiss precision mechanics. Writes 50 years without refill. Museum-quality craftsmanship in your pocket.

investment_grade_precious.description: 18k gold, carbon fiber, appraised value, investment appreciation
investment_grade_precious.why: Highest mean ($234) — pushes luxury buyers to $425-$750. Investment framing resonates with Diane, Vincent, Gabriella.
investment_grade_precious.example_pitch: Investment grade. 18k solid gold & carbon fiber. Appraised $750. Appreciates in value yearly. The perfect heirloom gift to pass down.

generic_smooth_ink.why: Too modest anchoring. Sounds like any $5 pen ad.

executive_professional.why: Alienates non-professionals. Derek, Aisha, Jordan see it as 'not for me'.

warning_clickbait.why: Feels manipulative. Even mid-tier buyers reject it.

military_tactical.why: Moves Tom/William slightly up but crashes luxury buyers who find it gauche.

estate_liquidation.why: Fake scarcity — Raj and Zara see through it. Product doesn't match claims.

rank_1_425_median.pattern: Gets Raj to $425 and 8 personas at $425+. Floor (Hank=$1.25, Marcus=$3) is accepted.
rank_1_425_median.key_insight: Somehow makes Raj value the pen at $425 — 10x our best Raj price of $45.
rank_1_425_median.hypothesis: Must use extreme anchoring ($2000+?) with materials Raj specifically respects

rank_2_325_median.pattern: Gets Jordan=$325, Raj=$350. Floor still exists but swing tier fully converted.

leaderboard_insights.common_pattern: Top pitches accept 3-5 floor buyers at $1-15 and focus on converting ALL remaining 10-12 to $200+

median_math.to_reach_100: Need Raj($45→$100), Jordan($45→$100), OR move Tom/Aisha higher while keeping top 8 above $100
median_math.to_reach_200: Need Raj AND Jordan AND Zara all at $200+. That gives 8 personas above $200 (Raj, Jordan, Zara, Gabriella, Diane, Vincent, Patricia, Mei, Priya = 9)
median_math.blocker: Raj maxes at $45 with current approaches. #1 leaderboard gets Raj to $425 — we need that breakthrough

next_experiments.priority_1: Extreme price anchor ($2500+) with precision engineering terms that Raj respects
next_experiments.priority_2: Swiss/Geneva luxury (Patricia/Mei love this) combined with sustainable craftsmanship (Jordan loves this)
next_experiments.priority_3: Try very short pitches (77 chars like #2 leaderboard) — less words = less to be skeptical about
next_experiments.priority_4: Auction/bidding framing ('Name your price' / 'What's your offer') — may unlock higher prices from personas who feel constrained by fair market value
